130: inline simdjson_result<value> array::at_pointer(std::string_view json_pointer) noexcept {
131:   if (json_pointer[0] != '/') { return INVALID_JSON_POINTER; }
132:   json_pointer = json_pointer.substr(1);
133:   // - means "the append position" or "the element after the end of the array"
134:   // We don't support this, because we're returning a real element, not a position.
135:   if (json_pointer == "-") { return INDEX_OUT_OF_BOUNDS; }
136: 
137:   // Read the array index
138:   size_t array_index = 0;
139:   size_t i;
140:   for (i = 0; i < json_pointer.length() && json_pointer[i] != '/'; i++) {
141:     uint8_t digit = uint8_t(json_pointer[i] - '0');
142:     // Check for non-digit in array index. If it's there, we're trying to get a field in an object
143:     if (digit > 9) { return INCORRECT_TYPE; }
144:     array_index = array_index*10 + digit;
145:   }
146: 
147:   // 0 followed by other digits is invalid

127: inline simdjson_result<value> object::at_pointer(std::string_view json_pointer) noexcept {
128:   if (json_pointer[0] != '/') { return INVALID_JSON_POINTER; }
129:   json_pointer = json_pointer.substr(1);
130:   size_t slash = json_pointer.find('/');
131:   std::string_view key = json_pointer.substr(0, slash);
132:   // Grab the child with the given key
133:   simdjson_result<value> child;
134: 
135:   // If there is an escape character in the key, unescape it and then get the child.
136:   size_t escape = key.find('~');
137:   if (escape != std::string_view::npos) {
138:     // Unescape the key
139:     std::string unescaped(key);
140:     do {
141:       switch (unescaped[escape+1]) {
142:         case '0':
143:           unescaped.replace(escape, 2, "~");
144:           break;
145:         case '1':
146:           unescaped.replace(escape, 2, "/");
147:           break;
148:         default:
149:           return INVALID_JSON_POINTER; // "Unexpected ~ escape character in JSON pointer");
150:       }
151:       escape = unescaped.find('~', escape+1);
152:     } while (escape != std::string::npos);
153:     child = find_field(unescaped);  // Take note find_field does not unescape keys when matching
154:   } else {
155:     child = find_field(key);
156:   }
157:   if(child.error()) {
158:     return child; // we do not continue if there was an error
159:   }
160:   // If there is a /, we have to recurse and look up more of the path
161:   if (slash != std::string_view::npos) {
162:     child = child.at_pointer(json_pointer.substr(slash));
163:   }
164:   return child;

301: simdjson_inline simdjson_result<value> document::at_pointer(std::string_view json_pointer) noexcept {
302:   rewind(); // Rewind the document each time at_pointer is called
303:   if (json_pointer.empty()) {
304:     return this->get_value();
305:   }
306:   json_type t;
307:   SIMDJSON_TRY(type().get(t));
308:   switch (t)
309:   {
310:     case json_type::array:
311:       return (*this).get_array().at_pointer(json_pointer);
312:     case json_type::object:
313:       return (*this).get_object().at_pointer(json_pointer);
314:     default:
315:       return json_pointer[0] != '/' ? INVALID_JSON_POINTER : NO_SUCH_FIELD;
316:   }
317: }
</source_slice>

378: inline simdjson_result<element> element::at_pointer(std::string_view json_pointer) const noexcept {
379:   SIMDJSON_DEVELOPMENT_ASSERT(tape.usable()); // https://github.com/simdjson/simdjson/issues/1914
380:   switch (tape.tape_ref_type()) {
381:     case internal::tape_type::START_OBJECT:
382:       return object(tape).at_pointer(json_pointer);
383:     case internal::tape_type::START_ARRAY:
384:       return array(tape).at_pointer(json_pointer);
385:     default: {
386:       if(!json_pointer.empty()) {
387:         return json_pointer[0] == '/' ? NO_SUCH_FIELD : INVALID_JSON_POINTER;
388:       }
389:       // an empty string means that we return the current node
390:       dom::element copy(*this);
391:       return simdjson_result<element>(std::move(copy));
392:     }
393:   }
</source_slice>
